text:
I've been thinking about what happened, and I owe you a real apology. I'm sorry. Not a quick, dismissive sorry, but a genuine one, because I know that what I said hurt you, and you didn't deserve that.

I didn't mean for my words to land the way they did, but I also know that good intentions don't erase the sting of feeling dismissed or disrespected. What matters is how you felt, and you felt hurt. That's real, and I take responsibility for it.

I value you more than I apparently showed in that moment. You've always been someone I care about, and the thought that I made you feel otherwise is something I genuinely regret. You deserved better from me, and I wish I had handled things differently.

I'm not asking you to forget it, or to pretend it didn't happen. I just want you to know that I've sat with this, that I understand why it stung, and that I'm committed to doing better. You can take all the time you need. I'll be here.
